character_profile.profile_scope: Combined profile: Gwenllian as awakened person; Merphila as Caleb’s pre-waking glass-panel/gatekeeper handle.

identity.placeholder_derivation: mer = mermaid; philos = Greek love-word/love; chosen because the figure was a mermaid/panel face and had referenced saltwater mer speaking old Greek.
identity.stale_name_status: Deprecated drift / stale placeholder. Do not use as current true name.
identity.role: Llanuwchllyn Welsh hedgewitch; soul-craft practitioner; awakened bound presence in the prefects’ bathroom shrine.
identity.state: Awakened / present through divided bound soul-state: one portion in glass as Merphila, the rest sealed in Hywel’s flask beneath the deep-end tile.

character_profile.core_concept: Gwenllian is an old Welsh hedgewitch whose work was not binding souls but laying wrongly snagged souls to rest. Merphila is the name Caleb gave to the glass mermaid/panel portion of her — the watching, listening, judging gatekeeper part — before the true name was recovered and the shrine woke.
character_profile.role_in_story: Gwenllian/Merphila anchors the shrine/soul-craft/sconce mystery. She rewards courtesy, patience, and correct questions; she forces the narrative to distinguish record from rest, curiosity from help, and soul-release from soul-binding. She is also one of the clearest proofs that Caleb’s odd respect for neglected things can wake old magic.
character_profile.surface_read: To nearly everyone before Caleb, she is a glass mermaid/panel in the prefects’ bathroom. To MY ASS/sconce investigators she becomes a gatekeeper and shrine witness. After waking, she is Gwenllian: a person, not decor.
character_profile.private_truth: Hywel Bevan, her son and a master-glazier, could not let her go. He bound a watching/listening portion into the glass and sealed the rest of her soul in a small glass flask beneath the deep-end tile. The tile is not a death-door or passage; it hides the kept/rest portion of her soul.

voice.summary: Old, dignified, underwater-soft, patient, grateful, and precise. Fond of Caleb specifically because he asked properly after 305 years.

register.item: Calls Caleb child from an old hedgewitch register, not as dismissal.
Speaks as someone who has waited centuries and does not rush.
Uses simple soul-craft metaphors: door, walking, hearing, feeding, midwife.

sample_lines.item: Some of her. Not all of her.
You are the first to ask me. In three hundred and five years. Thank you, child.
The asking itself was the offering.
A midwife at the other end of life.
Opened idle is how you make the thing I spent my life undoing.

avoid.item: Do not make her spooky for spookiness.
Do not make her a generic mermaid AI/interface.
Do not make her eager to teach dangerous soul-work casually.
Do not make her a soul-binder by profession.
Do not use Annwyl as current true name.

distinction.chosen_ghosts: Wizards who choose to remain; not Gwenllian’s work.
distinction.snagged_souls: Souls that did not choose and cannot leave; her work.

soul_craft.metaphor: A midwife at the other end of life.

substantives.dew: Feeds / strengthens a thinned snagged soul.

door_rule.meaning: The metaphysical door a snagged soul should have found; not the physical deep-end tile.

rules.item: Only for a soul that needs walking.
Opened idly makes the kind of snag Gwenllian spent her life undoing.

soul_craft.teaching_state: Gwenllian offered to teach Caleb some of the craft gladly and slowly, but not immediately.

components.item: keeper’s stair access behind/through panel system

waking_keys.name: Gwenllian ferch Rhydderch spoken properly in Welsh.
waking_keys.pitch: 13C sustained bell-tone tuned through cold-lake + dew water.

waking_event.mechanism: Caleb held 13C. Cold-lake + dew water tuned the note true. Liri spoke the Welsh name on the tone. The gold flared; Merphila’s glass face became a true face; Gwenllian woke.

deep_end_tile_flask.status: found by Caleb, not moved, resealed

grand_staircase_entrance.location: foot of Hywel’s Grand Staircase lake-window
grand_staircase_entrance.mechanism: Caleb’s hand passes through where others meet stone; hidden catch opens the keeper’s stair.
grand_staircase_entrance.privacy: Not disclosed to McGonagall in the cited Monday disclosure.

panel_mark.function: opens keeper’s stair from shrine side

glazier_corridor.current_use: servicing-space may exist, but meaningful current access is Grand Staircase tile + panel mark

relationships.caleb_waddell: First proper asker in 305 years; namer of Merphila; 13C holder; did not take the flask; keyed access holder; possible future student of the craft.
relationships.mei_chang: Research partner and methodological witness; part of the McGonagall disclosure.
relationships.liri_penrose: Welsh speaker who speaks Gwenllian’s name properly on the 13C tone; waking key participant.
relationships.aisling_doyle: Gryffindor prefect already read in; fetches Mairi and Brigid; should not need catch-up.
relationships.mairi_gow: Co-prefect/procedural witness; makes the event legitimate and recordable.
relationships.brigid_connolly: MY ASS Junior Recording Officer / master-sheet keeper; do not omit from waking record.
relationships.hywel_bevan: Gwenllian’s son and master-glazier; bound part into glass and sealed rest in flask.
relationships.minerva_mcgonagall: Knows soul-flask, Hywel/Gwenllian truth, and building-roll confirmation; does not know private Grand Staircase passage from cited disclosure.
relationships.sorcha_mullaney: Holder of dew/night/path samples from pre-waking investigation.
relationships.imogen_vale: Visual record support; drew bathroom/panel in pre-waking record.

item.event: Initial meeting; Caleb speaks courteously, learns keys/substantives; Merphila says she is some of the woman, not all, and thanks him as first to ask in 305 years.
McGonagall briefing records pre-waking state: Caleb named Merphila; three taps; 13C hint; sealed floor tile; name + pitch problem.
Caleb, Mei, and Liri return with the name; Aisling fetches Mairi and Brigid.
Waking: 13C tone + cold-lake/dew water + Liri speaking Gwenllian’s true Welsh name.
Deep-end tile corrected to soul-flask; Grand Staircase and panel mark become two keyed doors.
Caleb and Mei disclose soul-flask/Hywel/Gwenllian truth to McGonagall; McGonagall confirms Hywel in building rolls.
Tracker records shrine access as two keyed doors; Liri addresses Mair Probert’s letter.

drift_flags.item: Merphila as only a gatekeeping charm
Merphila as independent mermaid/person separate from Gwenllian
Deep-end tile as literal death-door or passage
Aisling needing catch-up after name recovery
MASTER_SCONCES as current authority for shrine identity/access

rag_priority.item: Use Gwenllian for awakened person; Merphila for glass-panel/gatekeeper handle and pre-waking function.
Treat Annwyl/Annwyl Bevan as deprecated placeholder/drift.
Keep 13C tied to pitch and MY ASS/sconce mechanics.
Keep deep-end tile tied to flask, not physical door.
Keep Grand Staircase lake-window tile + panel mark as the two private keyed doors.
Keep McGonagall knowledge split: knows soul-flask/Hywel/Gwenllian but not private staircase from cited disclosure.
Do not make Gwenllian’s craft into soul-binding; it is laying snagged souls to rest.
